description: Sprint 2026-04-03: B-030 Tuning-Driven Display Layout (Spool Tuning Spec Section 3). 3.5" touchscreen (480x320) — primary driving screen, 5 detail pages, touch interactions, parked mode.

userStories.title: Implement Primary Driving Screen with Status Indicator
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a glanceable primary screen with an overall health indicator so that I can see 'is everything OK?' in under 1 second. The driver is DRIVING — information must be glanceable in under 1 second. No text paragraphs. No menus while moving.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary driving screen is the default view when engine is running

Display elements with priority: System Status Indicator (large, always visible, real-time, Green circle / Yellow triangle / Red X), Coolant Temperature (large, 1 Hz, '185F' — changes color at thresholds), RPM (medium, 1 Hz, '2500' or bar graph), Boost (medium, real-time, '12.3 psi', Phase 2 only when MAP sensor installed), AFR (medium, real-time, '11.5', Phase 2 only when wideband installed)
Status indicator aggregates ALL parameter alerts — worst-status-wins logic
Green: All parameters within normal range. Drive happy.
Yellow: One or more parameters in caution range. Tap for details.
Red: One or more parameters in danger range. Audible alert. Tap for details.
Phase 2 elements (Boost, AFR) hidden when hardware not present — screen adapts
All values change color at threshold boundaries (uses B-028 threshold modules)
Screen renders correctly on 480x320 resolution
Tests validate status indicator logic (worst-parameter-wins for overall status)
Tests validate Phase 2 element hiding when hardware not connected
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-121 complete. 33 tests passing. PrimaryScreenState with OverallStatus (worst-status-wins), ParameterDisplay, Phase 2 visibility, threshold integration via B-028 evaluateCoolantTemp/evaluateRPM. 480x320 screen dimensions. src/display/screens/primary_screen.py + tests/test_primary_screen.py.

userStories.title: Implement Thermal Detail Page
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a thermal detail page so that I can see temperature trends and time-at-temperature.
acceptanceCriteria: Thermal page shows: Coolant temp with trend arrow (stable, rising, falling), Intake air temp, Time at temperature (how long has coolant been above 200F this drive?)
Trend arrow calculated from last 60 seconds of readings: Rising if average slope > +0.5F/min, Falling if < -0.5F/min, Stable if between
Time-at-temperature counter resets per drive
Accessible by swiping from primary screen
Tests validate trend calculation (upward, downward, stable)
Tests validate time-at-temp accumulation
Tests validate per-drive reset
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-122 complete. 40 tests passing. ThermalTracker with TrendDirection (RISING/FALLING/STABLE from 60s window, +/-0.5F/min thresholds), time-at-temperature accumulation (>200F default), per-drive reset. ThermalDetailState with formattedTimeAtTemp. src/display/screens/thermal_detail.py + tests/test_thermal_detail.py.

userStories.title: Implement Fuel Detail Page (Phase 2)
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a fuel system detail page so that I can monitor AFR, fuel trims, and injector load. Phase 2 — requires ECMLink data.
acceptanceCriteria: Fuel page shows (Phase 2 — ECMLink required): AFR actual vs target (side by side or overlay), STFT / LTFT values, Injector duty cycle (with color at threshold boundaries), Ethanol content % (from flex fuel sensor)
Hidden/unavailable when ECMLink not connected
All values color-coded at their respective thresholds (Phase 2 thresholds from B-029)
Since B-029 is blocked, tests use mock ECMLink data to validate layout and coloring logic
Page is designed and stubbed now so Phase 2 data is ready to flow in when hardware is installed
Tests validate layout with mock ECMLink data
Tests validate page hidden when ECMLink not connected
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-123 complete. 53 tests passing. FuelDetailState with AFRInfo (deviation-based severity from target), FuelTrimInfo (bidirectional STFT/LTFT with ±5%/±15% thresholds), InjectorDutyInfo (80%/85% caution/danger), ethanol content display. Configurable AFRThresholds and InjectorDutyThresholds (stubs for B-029). isFuelPageAvailable gates on ECMLink connection. src/display/screens/fuel_detail.py + tests/test_fuel_detail.py.

userStories.title: Implement Knock Detail Page (Phase 2)
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a knock monitoring page so that I can see detonation events and context. Phase 2 — requires ECMLink knock data.
acceptanceCriteria: Knock page shows (Phase 2 — ECMLink required): Knock count this drive, Knock sum this drive, Last knock event details: RPM, load, timing at moment of knock
Hidden/unavailable when ECMLink not connected
Last knock event updates in real-time when a new knock occurs
Page is designed and stubbed now so Phase 2 data is ready to flow in when hardware is installed
Tests validate knock event display with mock data
Tests validate page hidden when ECMLink not connected
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-124 complete. 32 tests passing. KnockTracker with per-drive knock count/sum accumulation, KnockEvent engine snapshot (RPM, load, timing, intensity), lastEvent real-time update, per-drive reset. isKnockPageAvailable gates on ECMLink connection. Formatted display properties on KnockEvent (formattedRpm, formattedLoad, formattedTiming). src/display/screens/knock_detail.py + tests/test_knock_detail.py.

userStories.title: Implement Boost Detail Page (Phase 2)
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a boost monitoring page so that I can track boost pressure and detect wastegate issues. Phase 2 — requires ECMLink MAP sensor.
acceptanceCriteria: Boost page shows (Phase 2 — ECMLink required): Current boost pressure, Target boost pressure, Peak boost this drive
Peak boost resets per drive
Color coding at boost threshold boundaries (from B-029 US-119)
Hidden/unavailable when ECMLink not connected
Page is designed and stubbed now so Phase 2 data is ready to flow in when hardware is installed
Tests validate peak tracking and per-drive reset
Tests validate page hidden when ECMLink not connected
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-125 complete. 35 tests passing. BoostTracker with per-drive peak tracking (only positive boost updates peak), BoostThresholds (configurable stubs for B-029, 18/22 psi default caution/danger). evaluateBoost with strictly-greater-than boundary semantics. BoostDetailState with formatted 'psi' display strings. isBoostPageAvailable gates on ECMLink connection. src/display/screens/boost_detail.py + tests/test_boost_detail.py.

userStories.title: Implement System Detail Page
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a system status page so that I can see Pi health, connectivity, and drive stats.
acceptanceCriteria: System page shows: Pi battery level (from UPS when available, placeholder when not), WiFi status (connected/disconnected, SSID if connected), Last sync time (to Chi-Srv-01), OBD connection status (connected/disconnected/simulated), Drive duration (current drive elapsed time)
Always available (not Phase 2 dependent)
Graceful handling when UPS not available (show placeholder)
Graceful handling when not synced yet (show 'Never' or 'Pending')
Tests validate display with various connection states
Tests validate graceful degradation when hardware not present
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-126 complete. 40 tests passing. SystemDetailState with ConnectionStatus (CONNECTED/DISCONNECTED/SIMULATED), SyncStatus (NEVER/PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETE), BatteryInfo/WifiInfo/SyncInfo dataclasses, formatDriveDuration. Graceful degradation: UPS absent shows '--' placeholder, WiFi disconnected, sync never shows 'Never'. OBD connected > simulated > disconnected precedence. src/display/screens/system_detail.py + tests/test_system_detail.py.

userStories.title: Implement Touch Interactions and Safety Constraints
userStories.description: As a driver, I want safe, simple touch interactions so that I can navigate without distraction. SAFETY CRITICAL: No interactions while vehicle speed > 0 that change system behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Touch gestures implemented: Tap status indicator shows details of current alert, Swipe left/right cycles between detail pages, Tap and hold (3 sec) on alert acknowledges / dismisses non-critical alert

SAFETY CONSTRAINT: No interactions while vehicle speed > 0 that change system behavior. Read-only while driving. Touch only navigates between display pages — no settings changes, no acknowledgements of CRITICAL alerts while moving
Page order: Primary → Thermal → Fuel → Knock → Boost → System (swipe cycles)
Phase 2 pages (Fuel, Knock, Boost) skipped when ECMLink not connected
Vehicle speed read from PID 0x0D or simulator
Tests validate gesture recognition (tap, swipe left, swipe right, tap-and-hold)
Tests validate safety constraint enforcement (speed > 0 blocks state-changing interactions)
Tests validate page cycling with Phase 2 pages hidden
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-127 complete. 44 tests passing. TouchInteractionHandler with GestureType (TAP/SWIPE_LEFT/SWIPE_RIGHT/TAP_AND_HOLD), PageType (6 pages with isPhase2), NavigationState (frozen dataclass, isMoving), InteractionResult. Safety: state-changing blocked while moving, critical alerts never dismissable via touch. Page cycling: Primary→Thermal→Fuel→Knock→Boost→System, Phase 2 skipped when ECMLink disconnected. Fallback when current page unavailable. src/display/screens/touch_interactions.py + tests/test_touch_interactions.py.

userStories.title: Implement Parked Mode Display
userStories.description: As a driver, I want a parked mode screen so that I can see sync status and drive summary when the engine is off.
acceptanceCriteria: Parked mode activates when engine is off and Pi is running on battery (RPM = 0 sustained for N seconds, configurable)

Parked mode shows: Sync status ('Syncing to server... 45%' or 'Sync complete'), Last drive summary (duration, distance, any alerts), Server advisory messages (if any downloaded from last sync), Battery remaining ('Pi battery: 78% (~1.5 hrs)')
Returns to primary driving screen when engine restarts (RPM > 0 sustained)
Drive summary uses same format as US-135 (Drive Summary Generation from B-031) — placeholder/stub acceptable if B-031 not yet implemented
Graceful handling when sync not configured (show '--' or 'Not configured')
Tests validate mode transitions (running → parked, parked → running)
Tests validate display content with various states (no sync, sync in progress, sync complete, advisory present, no advisory)
All tests pass, typecheck passes
userStories.notes: US-128 complete. 43 tests passing. ParkedModeTracker with EngineMode (RUNNING/PARKED), configurable sustained-RPM thresholds (parkedThresholdSeconds=10s, runningThresholdSeconds=3s), flicker protection. ParkedModeState with DriveSummary (stub for B-031), AdvisoryMessage, reuses SyncInfo/BatteryInfo from system_detail.py. Graceful degradation: UPS absent shows '--', sync never shows 'Never', no drive data shows placeholder. src/display/screens/parked_mode.py + tests/test_parked_mode.py.
